---
title: "TU9 Open Access Monitoring with OpenAlex"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
source("scripts/site_helpers.R")
meta <- read_meta()
```

Open-access monitoring for the [TU9](https://www.tu9.de/) universities, built from [OpenAlex](https://openalex.org/) and snapshotted over time.
The headline is the open-access share on the corresponding-author lens — the fraction of a university's corresponding-author output that is open access.
That is the view which matches [OpenAPC](https://openapc.net/) and transformative-agreement accounting, and the one relevant to OA monitoring.

Each university is shown five ways: as the single OpenAlex institution; grouped with every institution in its own OpenAlex/ROR `lineage`, independently of any curation; grouped with its affiliated organisations the way the [Leiden Ranking](https://open.leidenranking.com/) defines (e.g. with its university hospital); and that same Leiden member set restricted to CWTS Core sources under two readings — works with at least one Core-source location, and the narrower set of works whose primary venue is a Core source.
See [Background](background.qmd) for method and sources and [Downloads](downloads.qmd) for the raw files.

Refreshed on a schedule and released under CC0.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
cat(sprintf(
  "*Last updated: **%s**. Covering **%s** OpenAlex institution entities incl. consolidated components over **%s** %s. Figures span corresponding-author works published **%s–%s**.*\n",
  meta$updated, meta$n_entities, meta$n_snapshots,
  if (meta$n_snapshots == 1) "snapshot" else "snapshots",
  meta$oa_period_start, meta$oa_period_end))
```

## Coverage of the alliance

```{r}
#| echo: false
alliance_summary_table(meta)
```

All five readings cover the same nine universities, so the totals are directly comparable.
Consolidating adds the weight-1 Leiden `component` affiliates and can therefore only raise a university's count; three universities have no such affiliates and consolidate to themselves.
The multi-institutional hierarchy reading adds OpenAlex/ROR's own descendant institutions instead, and can likewise only raise a university's count relative to the single-institution view — but it is a different, uncurated member set, so it is **not** guaranteed to fall between the single-institution and Leiden-consolidated figures; across the nine TU9 universities it exceeds the consolidated figure about as often as it falls short of it.
The two Core-source readings both draw on the consolidated member set: *primary venue* is contained in *any location*, and both are contained in the consolidated view.

The [Institutions](institutions/index.qmd) page breaks the same five readings down per university.
